setting.note: Moneyed London under Thatcher, seen by a young aesthete who lodges with a Tory MP's family. The map runs from the tall white houses and locked communal garden of a Notting Hill square out to the clubland of Westminster, the villas of Holland Park, and the night paths of Hampstead Heath.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Alan Hollinghurst, first published 2004. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Line of Beauty, Alan Hollinghurst (2004). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep red of a Tory rosette and old claret, the colours of the world Nick moves through without belonging to it. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
